title: quercus gambelii subsp. vreelandii
intro: Quercus gambelii subsp. vreelandii, also known as Gambel oak, is a subspecies of evergreen oak tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Quercus gambelii subsp. vreelandii include its ability to support immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and potentially lower cholesterol levels. Quercus gambelii subsp. vreelandii can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in traditional folk medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
